action: In Visual Studio Code, ensure the current open folder is 'Documents/CalculatorApp'. Then, in the Explorer pane, create a new folder named 'src' inside 'CalculatorApp'. Open the 'src' folder. Inside the 'src' folder, right-click and create a new file named 'calculator_app.py'. Confirm successful creation of the folder and file.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task requires ensuring that the open folder in Visual Studio Code is 'Documents/CalculatorApp'. In the Explorer pane, I see 'D: Career', 'JoblHertz_AIlVideo| OmniParser', and 'D:|', but I do not see 'Documents/CalculatorApp' as the open folder. To open the correct folder, I should click the 'Open Folder' button in the Explorer pane (Box ID 34).

previous action: I was instructed to click the 'Open Folder' button in the Explorer pane to open the correct folder. The current screen now shows a folder selection dialog, with the folder structure visible and 'Documents' and its subfolders listed. This indicates that the 'Open Folder' dialog has appeared as expected. The next step is to navigate to the 'CalculatorApp' folder inside 'Documents' and select it. I can see 'CalculatorApp' as a folder option, so I should double-click it to open it.

previous action: I was instructed to double-click the 'CalculatorApp' folder in the folder selection dialog. The current screen now shows the contents of the 'CalculatorApp' folder, and the folder path at the top indicates '/home/Documents/CalculatorApp'. This confirms that the 'CalculatorApp' folder is open in the dialog. The next step is to select this folder to open it in Visual Studio Code. To do this, I should click the 'Select Folder' button.

previous action: I was instructed to click the 'Select Folder' button to open 'CalculatorApp' in Visual Studio Code. The current screen now shows Visual Studio Code with the Explorer pane open, and the folder 'CalculatorApp' is visible and appears to be the open workspace. This confirms that the correct folder is open. The next step is to create a new folder named 'src' inside 'CalculatorApp'. In VS Code, this is typically done by clicking the 'New Folder' icon in the Explorer pane. I will look for the 'New Folder' button and click it.

previous action: I was instructed to click the 'New Folder' icon in the Explorer pane to create a new folder inside 'CalculatorApp'. The current screen now shows a text input field under 'CalculatorApp' in the Explorer pane, ready to type the new folder name. This is the expected behavior for creating a new folder in VS Code. The next step is to type the folder name 'src' into this input field.

previous action: I was instructed to type 'src' as the new folder name. The current screen shows 'src' as a new folder under 'CalculatorApp' in the Explorer pane, indicating that the folder was successfully created. The next step is to open the 'src' folder. In VS Code, this is typically done by clicking on the folder name in the Explorer pane. I see 'src' listed, so I will click on it to open it.

previous action: I was instructed to click on the 'src' folder in the Explorer pane to open it. The current screen shows that the 'src' folder is now highlighted/selected in the Explorer pane, indicating it is open. The next step is to create a new file named 'calculator_app.py' inside the 'src' folder. In VS Code, this is typically done by right-clicking on the 'src' folder and selecting the option to create a new file. I will right-click on the 'src' folder to bring up the context menu.

previous action: I was instructed to right-click on the 'src' folder in the Explorer pane to bring up the context menu for creating a new file. The current screen shows a context menu open next to the 'src' folder, which is the expected outcome. The next step is to select the option to create a new file. In VS Code, this is typically labeled as 'New File' and should be one of the top options in the context menu. I will click the 'New File' option in the context menu.

previous action: I was instructed to click the 'New File' option in the context menu after right-clicking the 'src' folder. The current screen now shows a text input field under the 'src' folder in the Explorer pane, ready to type the new file name. This is the expected behavior for creating a new file in VS Code. The next step is to type the file name 'calculator_app.py' into this input field.
